text.title: Nginx Production LB Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for production Nginx LB. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: `worker_processes auto;` and `worker_rlimit_nofile 65535;` MUST appear in `nginx.conf`. `worker_connections` ≥ 4096. Hard-coded `worker_processes 1;` in production is forbidden.
rule.rationale: `auto` matches one worker per CPU core; `worker_rlimit_nofile` allows the high FD count modern LBs need. A single worker wastes cores and serializes all I/O.

rule.statement: Each `upstream` block MUST include `zone <name> 64k;` (or larger) when multiple workers share state (sticky cookies, keepalive). Per-worker upstream state is forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Without a shared zone, every worker maintains its own view of backend health; recoveries and bans propagate slowly and inconsistently.

rule.statement: HTTP/1.1 `upstream` blocks MUST set `keepalive 32;` (or higher) AND in the `location` block: `proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Connection "";`. Without these, every request opens a fresh TCP connection.
rule.rationale: Per-request connection setup adds TCP + TLS handshake latency (10s of ms) and saturates ephemeral ports on busy LBs. Keepalive cuts that.

rule.statement: TLS listeners MUST configure `ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;` and `ssl_ciphers` from a modern Mozilla profile. `ssl_stapling on;` + `ssl_stapling_verify on;` MUST be set. TLS 1.0 / 1.1 are forbidden.

rationale.source: Mozilla TLS guidelines + Nginx ssl docs

rule.rationale: TLS 1.0 / 1.1 fail PCI-DSS scans. OCSP stapling removes the runtime CRL lookup latency on every TLS handshake.

rule.statement: Public-facing servers MUST declare `limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=req_per_ip:10m rate=10r/s;` AND `limit_conn_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=conn_per_ip:10m;`. Each `server` MUST use `limit_req` + `limit_conn` directives.
rule.rationale: Without rate limiting at the LB, a single misbehaving client can saturate the upstream pool; cookie-based abuse defeats per-app limits.

rule.statement: Every HTTPS server block MUST add: `Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload"`, `X-Frame-Options DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, `Permissions-Policy "geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()"`.

rationale.source: OWASP Secure Headers project + Mozilla observatory

rule.rationale: These headers are the cheap-to-add baseline that blocks the bulk of low-effort attacks (clickjacking, MIME-sniff, leaked Referer).
